title: Wall Chart I – Algorithm: Emergency assessment and management of NYI airway and breathing

sections.heading: Emergency Assessment & Management of Newborn (NYI) Airway & Breathing
learningObjectives: Recognize the immediate signs of airway compromise in a newborn.
Perform a rapid, systematic airway assessment (positioning, suction, clear obstruction removal).
Initiate appropriate airway interventions (e.g., head‑tilt‑chin‑lift, jaw thrust, use of a laryngeal mask or endotracheal tube).
Evaluate breathing effectiveness (respiratory rate, chest movement, oxygen saturation).
Apply basic ventilation techniques when spontaneous breathing is inadequate (bag‑valve‑mask, CPAP).
Understand when to transition from basic to advanced resuscitative measures (chest compressions, medication).
Document the assessment and interventions accurately for hand‑over and quality‑improvement.

subsections.title: 1. Airway Assessment
points: Check for visible obstruction (e.g., secretions, meconium).
Position the head in neutral or slight extension.
Suction if needed – oral then nasal.

subsections.title: 3. Airway Intervention
points: If obstruction persists – consider Magill forceps or laryngoscope.
Insert appropriate airway device (LMA, ET tube) if ventilation ineffective.

subsections.title: 4. Ventilation Support
points: Deliver 21–30 % oxygen initially; titrate based on SpO₂.
Consider CPAP if spontaneous breathing improves but is weak.

subsections.title: 5. Ongoing Re‑assessment
points: Re‑evaluate airway and breathing every 30 seconds.
Escalate to chest compressions if heart rate < 60 bpm despite adequate ventilation.

images.description: Full‑size algorithm wall chart for NYI airway and breathing assessment
images.altText: Wall chart illustration showing step‑by‑step emergency airway and breathing management for a newborn.
